description: A university rocketry team is offering payload space on their sounding rocket, 'Valetudo', to external science experiments. Using the design file `~/Documents/rockets/ProjetoJupiter_Valetudo_2019.ork`, analyze the performance impact of a new 'Science Payload' mass component added to the nose cone. Determine the Apogee (m) and Maximum Velocity (Mach) for payload masses of 1.0 kg, 2.0 kg, 3.0 kg, and 4.0 kg. Export these findings to `~/Documents/exports/payload_curve.csv` using headers `Payload_Mass_kg,Apogee_m,Max_Mach`. Calculate the average apogee loss per kilogram using the formula `(Apogee at 1.0kg - Apogee at 4.0kg) / 3` and document the result in `~/Documents/exports/payload_summary.txt`. Save the modified design with the 4.0 kg payload to `~/Documents/rockets/valetudo_payload_4kg.ork`.
